commit.message:
Scale large images to fit viewport width

Added nearest-neighbor downscaling: images wider than 1000px are
scaled down proportionally at decode time and cached at the scaled
size. gfx_blit doesn't support scaling, so this is done in Oscan
before blitting.

commit.message:
fix: reconcile CronJob even when no pod uses the PVC

Previously, reconcileBackupResources returned early with NoPodFound
when no running pod had the PVC mounted, skipping the CronJob
reconciliation entirely. This prevented schedule/config changes from
being applied to existing CronJobs when the consumer pod was down.

Now the CronJob is always reconciled (created/updated) regardless of
pod presence. When no pod is found, node affinity is omitted (nil)
instead of setting an empty hostname, allowing the CronJob to run on
any node.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix multi-word links: one hint per link, not per word

Each word in an <a> tag was creating a separate LinkInfo entry, causing
'f' hint mode to show multiple labels for a single link. Now tracks
the bounding box across all words and emits one LinkInfo per link text
span. Multi-line links emit one entry per line.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: preserve immutable fields via Rust resolver and version cluster class names (#956)

Move immutable field resolution from Python to a generic Rust module
that reads existing K8s resources before rebuilding topology variables.
This prevents CAPI from overwriting immutable fields during updates.

- Add ResourceFieldMappings<T> generic system with typed K8s lookups
- Add FieldMapping with jsonptr-based field extraction and label fallback
- Add kopium-generated OpenStackCluster CRD types
- Add compile-time git-describe via vergen-gix for cluster class naming
- Add compile-time assertion that VERGEN_GIT_DESCRIBE is non-empty
- Add PyO3 error boundary in driver.rs (create_exception + From<Error>)
- Add 15 unit tests for field resolution, 2 for cluster class naming
- Remove redundant Python integration tests covered by Rust tests
- Make rust_driver a required parameter in Cluster.__init__
- Update CI to use full git history for vergen-gix tag resolution

Signed-off-by: Mohammed Naser <mnaser@vexxhost.com>
Co-authored-by: Mohammed Naser <mnaser@vexxhost.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Universal agent primitives: search, read, write, link

Replaces the need for dozens of specialized tools with 4 composable
primitives that let the agent handle any lawyer workflow:

MatterStore (src/agent/matter-store.ts):
- Unified queryable store for all matter entities
- Matter-namespaced keys prevent cross-matter data access
- Full-text search across titles, content, and metadata
- Link validation ensures both endpoints exist in same matter
- Per-request isolation (no global singleton leaking across users)

Agent primitives:
- search_matter: full-text search across all entity types in a matter
- read_entity: get/list/get_links for any entity by type and ID
- write_entity: create or update any entity with type validation
- link_entities: typed bidirectional connections between entities

MCP server (src/mcp/litigation-server.ts):
- All 4 primitives registered as MCP tools alongside existing tools
- Any MCP client gets the same universal capabilities as the chat agent

System prompt updated to teach compose-from-primitives approach:
- Agent uses primitives for arbitrary workflows
- Specialized tools (conflicts, deadlines, Bates) kept for validated computation

Security fixes from GPT-5.4 review:
- Matter-namespaced entity keys in store
- Link validation (both endpoints must exist in same matter)
- Delete scoped to matter (won't remove other matters' links)
- Search metadata indexed for completeness

Agent module exports updated with new types and functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(orchestrator): add automations system and background agent sessions

Automations system (automations.rs):
- AutomationRegistry: register, list, enable/disable, remove automations
- AutomationRunner: execute automation with trigger event context
- CronScheduler: tokio-based cron scheduler with basic pattern parsing
- WebhookListener: HTTP endpoint handler for webhook triggers
- Slash command helpers for /automate list|add|run|enable|disable
- Persistence to .caduceus/automations.json
- Pre-built templates: nightly-tests, pr-review, dependency-update

Background agent sessions (background.rs):
- BackgroundAgentManager: start/pause/resume/cancel/status/list
- Cooperative pause via CancellationToken
- SQLite persistence for crash recovery
- Slash command helpers for /background start|list|status|cancel|pause|resume

Adds 25 tests covering registry CRUD, persistence, cron parsing,
webhook routing, background lifecycle, and slash commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(input): bracketed paste + typeahead capture during streaming

Issue 1: Enable rustyline bracketed paste mode so pasted multi-line
text is inserted as a single buffer instead of triggering Enter on
each newline.

Issue 2: The raw-mode keyboard listener during streaming was consuming
all non-Esc keystrokes silently. Replace EscListenerGuard/spawn_esc_listener
with StreamInputGuard/spawn_stream_input which buffers printable
characters typed during AI streaming and exposes them via stop_and_take().
The main REPL loop now pre-fills readline with any typeahead text after
streaming ends. Also fixes Ctrl+C during streaming (was silently
swallowed before; now correctly triggers abort).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix image rendering: strip w/h header from pixel array

img_load() returns [width, height, pixel0, pixel1, ...] but gfx_blit()
expects just pixels. The old code passed the full array, causing the
width/height values to be interpreted as pixel data (random colors).

Now the cache strips the 2-element header at decode time and stores
pixel-only arrays with dimensions in parallel arrays.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
forecast: add thread-safe model runtime cache

Add ModelCache with TTL expiry, LRU eviction, and per-key init
locks that prevent duplicate concurrent loads. Provides
get_or_load() returning (model, metadata) with cache hit/miss
reporting.

Addresses: pretrained model loading inefficiency, foundation
model I/O overhead, concurrency risk, and GluonTS training-on-
the-fly dedup. Single module-level singleton for process-wide
reuse.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add MemoryDistillerAgent for post-completion memory distillation (#335)

* Add MemoryDistillerAgent and memory distillation on completed AgentProcess

- Create MemoryDistillationResult record with stored/revoked memory lists
- Create MemoryDistillerAgent with @Agent, @Action, @AchievesGoal annotations
  using ai().withDefaultLlm().createObject() for LLM-based memory distillation
- Add maxMemories parameter to KafkaAgenticAggregateRuntime constructor
- Integrate memory distillation in resumeNextAgentTask: when AgentProcess
  finishes with COMPLETED status, run MemoryDistillerAgent to completion
  using run() and translate results into MemoryStoredEvent/MemoryRevokedEvent
- Enforce net memory limit: stored - revoked <= maxMemories - currentCount
- Handle gracefully: missing agent, null result, exceptions
- Add comprehensive tests for distillation logic and limit enforcement

- Rename maxMemories to maxTotalMemories and add maxMemoriesAdded in @AgenticAggregateInfo
- Use MemoryStoredEvent/MemoryRevokedEvent directly in MemoryDistillationResult (no intermediate DTOs)
- Rename agent names: DefaultAgent → "Default", MemoryDistillerAgent → "MemoryDistiller"
- Use multi-line String (text blocks) in MemoryDistillerAgent prompt building
- Change "not deployed" log level from DEBUG to WARN
- Pass both maxTotalMemories and maxMemoriesAdded to distiller bindings
- De-duplicate revocations by memoryId
- Add safe binding reads with instanceof checks
- Update all tests for new signatures and types

commit.message:
chore: remove optional files and development tooling

Remove non-essential files to streamline the repository:
- .editorconfig - Editor preferences (optional)
- .prettierignore - Prettier formatter exclusions (optional)
- xo.config.ts - Linter configuration (optional)
- .husky/ - Git pre-commit hooks (optional, can interfere with workflow)
- tests/ - Unit tests (optional for standalone bot)
- .github/ - GitHub issue templates and workflows (optional)

These files are not required for the bot to function. Users can configure
their own linting, formatting, and testing if needed.

Minimal dependencies remain: source code, package.json, tsconfig.json,
esbuild.config.mjs, and .env configuration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

check/record operations. Returns structured duplicate responses
containing the original outcome.

submission and record() after successful placement. No-op when
no idempotency key is provided.

commit.message:
fix: lower clan autocomplete minimum from 3 to 2 characters

Clan tags are often 2-4 characters (e.g. -K-, -N-, KA). The previous
3-character minimum prevented users from finding clans with short tags.

Frontend: make the query length gate mode-aware (2 for clan, 3 for
player). Backend: lower clan_name_suggestions threshold to 2 chars.
Player search stays at 3 since WoWS names are >= 3 chars.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

trading: add account-level risk gate

Add AccountRiskLimits model with min_margin_level_pct,
max_floating_loss, and max_total_exposure_lots thresholds.
All disabled by default, preserving existing behavior.

_evaluate_account_risk_gate() checks account state against limits
and returns structured violations or None when clear. Handles
missing account_info gracefully (skips checks that need it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: combo subscriptions — isCombo flag, comboComponents @ManyToMany, estimatedShipping, actualShipping on SubscriptionMonth

- Subscription: isCombo, estimatedShipping, comboComponents (self-referential ManyToMany, join table subscription_combo_component), virtual comboComponentIds getter
- SubscriptionMonth: actualShipping field for expense tracking
- AdminController: applySubBody handles isCombo, estimatedShipping, comboComponentIds (prevents combos of combos)
- Frontend: SubscriptionInlineForm — combo toggle, component picker (non-combo subs only), min 2 validation
- Frontend: CompanyDetailView — COMBO badge, component names, shipping column
- Frontend: availableComponents prop passed from CompanyFormPage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(expenses): fix NOT NULL constraint crash when saving manual/reduction expenses

Root cause: explicit null values for NOT NULL DEFAULT columns bypass PostgreSQL DEFAULT:
  - extraction_warnings: NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::text[]
  - requires_user_confirmation: NOT NULL DEFAULT false

These were set to: input.x ?? null — which inserts explicit NULL even when the
caller doesn't provide the field. PostgreSQL only uses DEFAULT when the column is
OMITTED from the INSERT, not when NULL is explicitly given.

Fix: use spread pattern to omit when value is absent, letting DB DEFAULT apply.
Same pattern already applied to cost_type in migration 122 fix.

Also:
- Add reductionError state + error display in reduction form (was silently failing)
- Clear reduction error on cancel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
trading: add slippage-to-deviation conversion helper

Add _resolve_slippage_to_deviation() that converts user-facing
slippage_pips into MT5 deviation (points). Handles 3/5-digit
brokers (10 points/pip) and 2/4-digit brokers (1 point/pip).

Precedence: explicit deviation > slippage_pips > default (20).
Returns structured metadata showing conversion source and
parameters for auditability.

commit.message:
Remove stale quote fallback — show missing-time message instead

When no quote exists for the current minute, always show the
'Hmm, looks like...' message. Previously the display would re-use
the last valid quote, masking the gap. Removed s_last_quote and
s_has_last_quote state entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(kernels): dispatch per headdim via HEADDIM constexpr

Replace the runtime `headdim` parameter in both kernels with
`HEADDIM: tl.constexpr`.  Triton JIT compiles a separate PTX binary
for every distinct HEADDIM value — this is literal kernel dispatch per
head dimension.

Benefits per specialisation:
- Static loop unrolling: d-block loops change from `range(0, n_dblocks)`
  to `tl.static_range(HEADDIM // BLOCK_D)` with compile-time bounds,
  enabling full unrolling and optimal instruction scheduling.
- Dead-load elimination: the forward kernel previously loaded q_d1/q_d2/q_d3
  unconditionally before the K-block loop (all-zero masked for HEADDIM=64
  but the instructions were still issued); constexpr unrolling removes them.
- Mask elimination: `offs_d_cur < HEADDIM` is always True for aligned
  headdims (64/128/256 with BLOCK_D=64), so the predicate is dropped from
  every load/store in both kernels.
- Independent autotune caches: key now includes HEADDIM so each head-dim
  value gets its own independently tuned BLOCK_M/BLOCK_N/BLOCK_D config.

Forward kernel: restructured pre-loop Q loading from a manual 4-way
(q_d0/q_d1/q_d2/q_d3) pattern to a `q_blocks` tuple built with
`tl.static_range`; same for K loading + dot product accumulation inside
the K-block loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
trading: add configurable pre-trade safety policy

Add TradeSafetyPolicy model with opt-in rails: max_volume,
require_stop_loss, max_deviation, and reduce_only. All fields
default to disabled, preserving existing behavior.

_evaluate_safety_policy() checks each active rail and returns
structured violations list or None when clear. Supports multiple
simultaneous violations for clear error reporting.

Ready for consumer adoption — not yet wired into order paths.
Consumers pass a policy instance to gate requests before
submission.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use authorize_redirect for Google OAuth to preserve session state

Previously the login endpoint returned a JSON URL, which broke the
session cookie flow (CSRF state mismatch on callback).

- backend: authorize_redirect() stores state in session on port 8000
- frontend: direct window.location redirect instead of fetch + redirect
